title: Microsoft Rolls Out Media Center
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: News analysis of Microsoft's announcement of Windows XP Media Center Edition — a living-room PC variant combining TV tuner, hardware video encoder, and infrared remote, first demoed at CES 2002 under the 'Freestyle' codename. HP, NEC, and Samsung are the launch OEMs; Kastner frames the offering as the strongest PC-in-the-living-room push to date while noting Windows task-scheduling vulnerabilities and competition from SonicBlue and TiVo.
importance_rationale: Documents the 2002 launch of Windows XP Media Center Edition — a seminal step toward convergence of PC and living-room entertainment.
relevance_rationale: Foundational context for home-theater-PC and later streaming-box trajectories; still referenced in PC convergence histories.
prescience_rationale: Kastner flagged Media Center as the 'strongest push to date' into the living room; multi-vendor MCE PC adoption followed through 2003-2008 and seeded today's HTPC and streaming-device market.
